criteria_verification.criterion_text: Method `build_for(stage: StageClass, build_id: str, feature_id: str | None) -> list[ContextEntry]`

criteria_verification.evidence: StageLogReader Protocol's get_approved_stage_entry method is approved-only by contract: there is no flag for the builder to widen the filter. Covered by TestApprovedOnly (3 tests) including no-prior-entry, in-progress (returns None by Protocol contract), and a check that the builder does not bypass the filter.
criteria_verification.notes: StageLogReader Protocol's get_approved_stage_entry method is approved-only by contract: there is no flag for the builder to widen the filter. criteria_verification.criterion_text: Refuses to return any entry whose underlying artefact path falls
criteria_verification.evidence: _build_path_entries calls WorktreeAllowlist.is_allowed for every path; rejects are filtered out individually with a structured WARNING log line including build_id, path, and recipe description. Covered by TestAllowlistEnforcement (3 tests) including allowed-vs-denied mix, text-bypasses-allowlist, and single-path-rejected.
criteria_verification.notes: _build_path_entries calls WorktreeAllowlist.is_allowed for every path; rejects are filtered out individually with a structured WARNING log line including build_id, path, and recipe description. Covered by TestAllowlistEnforcement (3 tests) including allowed-vs-denied mix, text-bypasses-allowlist, and single-path-rejected.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: For per-feature stages, scopes the lookup to that feature's prior
criteria_verification.evidence: build_for forwards feature_id into the StageLogReader call when the producer is in PER_FEATURE_STAGES; per-feature consumer + missing feature_id yields an empty list (safe-default). Covered by TestPerFeatureScoping (3 tests) including FEAT-1/FEAT-2 isolation, per-feature-consumer-with-non-per-feature-producer, and missing-feature_id misuse.

- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.
